D6 Display L3 Apply Atom

The ColorChecker Classic

twenty-four named patches — skin tones, primaries, and a neutral ramp — the reference chart every camera and display is judged against

The X-Rite (Macbeth) ColorChecker is twenty-four matte patches in a 6×4 grid: natural colours like skin and foliage, additive and subtractive primaries, and a six-step neutral grey ramp. Because their values are standardised, the chart is the common yardstick for camera profiling, white balance, and display accuracy.

Step the selector to inspect each patch by name and its standard sRGB value. The bottom row is the neutral ramp used to check grey balance; the colour patches probe hue and saturation accuracy across the gamut.
